# Cold Calling # Cold Calling ---
🚨 **Why Cold Calling?** Cold calling lets you have real conversations with decision makers instead of waiting for them to open your email. If you want to add another channel to your outreach or build a true multi-channel acquisition system, this section has everything you need.
## What This Section Covers --- ✅ When cold calling makes sense for your situation ✅ The most cost-effective cold calling tech stack ✅ Scripts and templates for you to use ✅ Video training from CCG members who've done it --- ## Should I Add Cold Calling? --- [Should I Add Cold Calling?](/resources/should-i-add-cold-calling-2edf6d94771f8125ad17d29bde4673d2) ## Cold Calling Tech Stack (Outsource or DIY) ---
ℹ️ Tools to run high-volume cold calling at 1/10th the cost. Outsource the whole thing or do it yourself with cheaper tools.
[Cold Calling Tech Stack](/resources/cold-calling-tech-stack-2edf6d94771f8146906cc36c907e08c5) ## Scripts & Templates --- [Scripts & Templates](/resources/scripts-templates-2edf6d94771f81a6b853c0bda530e96b) ## Conquer Cold Calling Course ---
⚠️ **Note:** This course was created in 2021. The tools mentioned may be outdated, but the fundamentals of cold calling (mindset, call structure, handling objections, closing) are timeless.
[Conquer Cold Calling Course](/resources/conquer-cold-calling-course-2edf6d94771f81899acaf96441d2f148) ## Cold Calling Training Videos ---
💡 These videos teach timeless cold calling principles. Combine with the modern tech stack above for best results.
[Cold Calling Training Videos](/resources/cold-calling-training-videos-2edf6d94771f812b8e30e44040743924) ## Want To Hire Your Own SDR? --- If you want to hire and manage your own cold calling team instead of outsourcing, we've got a full guide with job posting templates and interview frameworks. key-resources/recommended-settings.title: Recommended Settings
key-resources/recommended-settings.content:
# Recommended Settings
ℹ️ **This page contains our current recommended settings for cold email infrastructure.** **Last Updated:** March 2025 Bookmark this page and check back regularly for updates.
--- ## Quick Reference Table | **Setting** | **CCG Recommendation** | | --- | --- | | **Inbox Split** | 50% Google / 50% Outlook | | **Inboxes per Domain** | 1 inbox per domain | | **Warmup Duration** | 4 days only | | **Daily Send Limit** | 25 emails/day per inbox (after ramp-up) | | **Warmup Tool** | Built-in (Instantly/SmartLead) | --- ## Initial Warmup Settings (Before Sending)
⚠️ **We recommend warming domains for 4 days ONLY, then stopping warmup and starting campaigns.**
| **Setting** | **Value** | | --- | --- | | **Warmup Duration** | 4 days | | **Emails Ramp Up** | 4 per day | | **Reply Rate** | 80% | --- ## Sending Ramp-Up Schedule After warmup, gradually increase your sending volume: | **Days** | **Emails per Day per Inbox** | | --- | --- | | Days 1-3 | 5 emails | | Days 4-6 | 10 emails | | Days 7-9 | 15 emails | | Days 10-12 | 20 emails | | Days 13+ | 25 emails |
💡 **Tip:** Increase volume by adjusting the number of new leads in your campaign, not by changing the daily limit setting.
--- ## Campaign Settings Checklist - [ ] Send Emails as PLAIN TEXT ONLY - [ ] Auto Stop Sending Emails On Reply - [ ] Disable Open Tracking - [ ] Disable Link Tracking - [ ] Gap Between Emails: Minimum 5 minutes - [ ] Prioritise New Leads Over Follow-Ups - [ ] A/Z Testing: Reply Rate as Winning Metric - [ ] Enable Provider Matching --- ## Sending Schedule | **Setting** | **Recommendation** | | --- | --- | | **Time-Zone** | Match your lead list's time zone | | **Campaign Hours** | 8:00am - 7:00pm | | **Days To Send** | Weekdays ONLY | --- ## Alternative Settings (Extended Warmup)
ℹ️ If you prefer a longer warmup period, use these settings instead:
### Extended Warmup (14-21 Days) | **Setting** | **Value** | | --- | --- | | **Warmup Duration** | 14-21 days | | **Warmup per Day** | 40 | | **Ramp Up** | 5 per day | | **Randomise** | 15-40 | | **Reply Rate** | 35% | | **Daily Send Limit** | 20 emails/day per inbox | ### Warmup While Sending (2x Rule)
⚠️ **Important:** Warmup volume should be **2x your cold email volume**. If you're sending 20 cold emails/day, warmup should be 40/day.

# Choose Your Service Model

# Choose Your Service Model

---

### **1. Pay Per Lead**

**How It Works:**

Clients pay a set fee for each lead you generate. A lead can be defined as a lead showing interested in the offer, or agree to receive more information. 

**Pros:**

- Lot less operational hassle
- Low commitment for clients, making it easier to close deals.

**Cons:**

- Client’s can struggle to convert the leads, reducing their ROI
- Risk of misaligned expectations around lead quality.

---

### **2. Pay Per Call**

**How It Works:**

Clients pay for each sales call booked or attended.

**Pros:**

- Tied directly to high-intent actions, making it attractive to clients.
- Increased value allows you to charge more
- Best beginner model

**Cons:**

- Client’s may dispute billable calls
- Low show up rates reduces your earnings.
- Hard to scale after a certain point.

---

### **3. Revenue Share**

**How It Works:**

You take a percentage of the revenue generated from your efforts. Consider this if expected call quantity is on the low end, but CLTV is high.  

**Pros:**

- High earning potential if your efforts lead to significant revenue.
- Easiest service model to sell.

**Cons:**

- You income depends on your clients close rate, which may be out of your control.
- Client’s can withhold sharing information about closes - or stop paying you.
- Delayed payment as revenue takes time to materialize.

---

### **4. Retainers**

**How It Works:**

Clients pay a fixed monthly fee for your services.

**Pros:**

- Predictable income
- Less transactional, and fosters more long-term client relationships.
- Allows you to focus on results without constant performance pressure.

**Cons:**

- Requires strong client trust upfront.
- May feel less flexible to clients compared to pay-per-result models.

---

### **5. Selling Systems**

**How It Works:**

Clients pay a one-time fee for a system or framework you build, such as a lead-gen system or automated sales process. After that, you ensure cashflow by running it for them on a flat fee, give them monthly coaching or providing them with leads and scripts. 

**Pros:**

- High upfront revenue with minimal ongoing commitment.
- Highly scalable offer.

**Cons:**

- No recurring income unless paired with add-ons or maintenance fees.
- Must be competent in all aspects of lead generation to be able to sell.

---

### **6. Hybrid Models**

**How It Works:**

Combines elements of two or more pricing models. Most common hybrids are: 

- Base retainer + Performance-based bonus.
- Pay Per Call + Revenue Share of closed deals

**Pros:**

- Balances predictable income with high-earning potential.
- Aligns your goals with the client’s success while covering your costs.

**Cons:**

- Can be more complex to structure and communicate to clients.
- Requires detailed contracts to avoid misunderstandings.

# Set-Up Fees

Set-up fees are essential for covering the upfront costs of onboarding clients, building systems, and launching successful campaigns. Here’s how to structure them effectively.

### **Why Charge Set-Up Fees?**

1. **Covers Upfront Work:** Includes onboarding, tech setup, and strategy development.
2. **Shows Commitment:** Ensures clients are invested from the start.
3. **Balances Risk:** Mitigates your financial exposure when onboarding new clients.
4. **Cashflow** $$$

### **Common Types of Set-Up Fees**

1. One-Time Set-Up Fees
    1. A single, upfront payment covering all onboarding and launch costs.
2. Monthly Tech/Maintenance Fees
    1. Monthly fees to cover your costs and keep the systems running
    2. It’s easier to sign clients with this approach, but you may encounter clients who lack the same level of commitment compared to those paying higher one-time set-up fees.

# Service model based on your experience

## Beginner

For beginners, we recommend selling pay-per-performance. 

**Why?**

Because this is the ‘easiest’ service model to sell as there is very little risk and it’s pay-per-performance. This allows you to speed up the process of signing clients, collect case studies and then ultimately, charge more money. 

## Intermediate

Pay-per-performance OR retainer model. You can also create hybrid modules to fit your offer. 

## Expert

Any of the three models you wish to sell or a combination of different models

# Choosing A Niche # Choosing A **Niche** --- # **Start With Who You Want to Reach** If your service involves contacting your client’s prospects, think first about **who you’d like to reach out to** and what section of the market aligns with your skills and experience. Have experience in IT? Focus on that market—as you understand their platforms, terminology, and challenges. This familiarity can lead to better response rates. You can leverage that experience to select a niche in two ways: 1. Work directly with IT businesses. 2. Work with clients targeting IT businesses. # **Why Leverage Your Experience?** Because it gives you an edge. You already understand: - The problems IT businesses face. - Someone who experiences those problems firsthand. - Their terminology and how they communicate. - How they’re likely to respond to different outreach strategies. This insight lets you craft targeted messaging and solutions that resonate, giving you a significant advantage. # **Not Every Niche Is Worth It** Here’s the reality: A profitable niche is one where you can provide clear, measurable value. They need to want what you’re selling, and they need to have money to spend. Helping unemployed people land jobs by helping them write their resumes and practice job interviews is a starving market - but they won’t have the money to pay you. Furthermore; People only buy when what you’re offering is worth more to them than what they’re paying. ***For example:*** If you offer a $3,000 service, it needs to generate at least $12,000 in revenue (a 3x ROI) for the client. If you can’t make them that kind of return in 30-60 days, it’s unlikely they’ll buy, and the niche might not be viable for you. # **Top 5 steps to choosing a “good” market/niche** ---
**1.** The niche has a *painful* problem that you can actually solve
**2.** The market is growing. Eg. SaaS or Ecom
**3.** Your niche actually has money. Eg. you’re selling to doctors or $5M revenue a year companies instead of the unemployed
**4.** The niche is easy (or just not impossible) to reach
**5.** You preferably understand the niche more because either you worked in the industry or know people from the industry. This way you understand what their true pains are, what’s already working, how you can improve it and differentiate yourself ***(the most important step)***

# Creating Your VSL

# Creating Your VSL

---

A strong Video Sales Letter (VSL) boosts leads, appointments, and sales across all niches, from SEO to B2B services.

# Why a VSL?

**1. Transform Sales Calls**

- A great VSL pre-sells prospects, turning sales calls into easy, order-taking sessions. By addressing concerns upfront, prospects come prepared and ready to commit.

**2. Crowdsource Content**

- Shape your VSL using common questions from sales calls. This ensures you address key pain points in the exact sequence prospects expect, building trust and engagement.

**3. Cut Lead Costs**

- Answering questions in your VSL can reduce cost per lead by up to 70%. By filtering unqualified leads, you attract prospects aligned with your service and message.

**4. Overcome Camera Shynes**

- Being on camera might feel uncomfortable at first, but the boost in close rates and scalable sales make it worth the effort.

**5. Data-Driven Advantage**

- A VSL built on real sales data is proven to boost conversions. Aligning your message with actual prospect questions ensures relevance and effectiveness.

# **Action Plan**

- **Record Sales Calls:** Analyze 40-50 calls to identify recurring questions and objections.
- **Revisit Your ICP:** Revisit your ICP to deeply understand what makes them buy.
- **Script Your VSL:** Communicate your message clearly, showcase case studies and address common objections in a clear way.
- **Produce Your Video:** Confidently present the script in a high-quality video.

# **VSL Length**

The ideal length of your VSL depends on your offering and target audience. For a unique SaaS solution, a longer VSL may be necessary to fully explain its value. However, keep in mind that most viewers won’t watch the entire video, so keeping it concise is often more effective.

**General Guidelines:**

- **Cold Traffic:** Keep it under **3 minutes** to capture attention quickly.
- **Warm Traffic:** Aim for a maximum of **10 minutes** to provide more detail while retaining engagement.

# **Tech Stack**

- **Recording:** Use tools like **Loom** or **Wistia** to record your VSL with ease.
- **Script & Presentation:** Read from a well-structured **Google Doc**, **Gamma Presentation**, or **PowerPoint**—make sure it’s clear and easy to follow.
- **Visual Enhancements:** For added polish, hire someone on **Fiverr** to create professional visuals to elevate your VSL.

# Steven Trister’s Framework for Creating Effective VSLs

# Ideal Client Profile

# Ideal Client Profile

---

# **What Is an Ideal Client Profile (ICP)?**

An Ideal Client Profile (ICP) is a detailed description of the perfect customer for your business. Think of it as a blueprint for identifying companies that are most likely to benefit from and value your services.

Your ICP includes key details such as:

- **Type of Business:** What industry are they in?
- **Location:** Where are they based?
- **Size:** How big is their team?
- **Revenue:** What’s their annual income?
- **Decision-Makers:** Who makes the purchasing decisions?
- **Pain Points:** What challenges can your service solve for them?
- **Spending Habits:** How much do they typically invest in services like yours?

This profile helps you identify and attract clients that align perfectly with your offering, so you can focus on high-value opportunities.

# **Why Is an ICP Important?**

Having an ICP is essential for optimizing your agency’s success. Here's why:

- **Saves Time and Money:** Focus on the right prospects instead of wasting resources on leads that don’t fit.
- **Improves Service Quality:** Tailor your offerings to perfectly meet the needs of your ideal clients.
- **Increases Customer Satisfaction:** ICP-aligned clients are more likely to love your service and stay loyal.
- **Boosts Revenue:** Targeted outreach leads to more conversions, higher close rates, and bigger deals.

By knowing your ideal customer, you create a clearer path to business growth and better relationships.

# **What to Consider When Building Your ICP**

When creating your ICP, focus on specific traits and measurable criteria:

### **Demographics and Basics:**

- **Industry:** What niche or submarket do they belong to?
- **Location:** Which country or region are they in?
- **Size:** Team headcount or business scale.
- **Revenue:** Annual revenue range.

### **Behavior and Needs:**

- **Decision-Makers:** Who are the key players (e.g., CEO, CMO, VP of Sales)?
- **Pain Points:** What challenges or problems do they face that your service can solve?
- **Spending Habits:** Average deal size (ideally $3K or more) and lifetime value.

### **Market Insights:**

- **Close Rate:** How many leads turn into paying clients?
- **Market Size:** Is there enough demand in this segment to scale your business?
- **Case Studies:** Do you already have 2+ success stories in this niche?

### **Platforms and Channels:**

- **Where Are They Active?:** LinkedIn, email, specific forums, or other platforms they frequent.

# Market Research on Your ICP

Market research is the **SINGLE** biggest difference that separates average campaigns from excellent campaigns. Time spent better understanding your market is never wasted. Once you truly understand their pain points and how you solve them, your emails write themselves. 

[Market Research](/resources/market-research-c09cc891879442c69e6a15d38103af34)

# **Be as specific as you can**

If more than 20% of the prospects you get on a call with are 100% qualified, there’s something wrong with your ICP. 

Make it more specific and only agree to work with people who you’re CERTAIN you’ll be able to get results for.

## An Example ICP

- Niche - B2B SaaS companies offering Enterprise Software
- Average Annual Deal size - $10,000+
- Close rate - 20% + on warmed appts
- Minimum 2 outcome-focused case studies (in the specific target market they are targeting)
- Website optimized for a specific target market (not a general, bullshit-filled website from 2005)
- Total Addressable Marketing - 10K+
- Unique Selling Proposition - Eg: Proprietary Software, Proprietary Systems Unique to them, Guarantee, Purely Performance-Based Offer, Free Value Add Front End
- Revenue of $3M per year / 10-150 employees
- Companies in the US, UK, Canada
- C level & senior executives (brand director, head of brand, head of branding, brand manager, marketing technology lead, marketing tech lead, marketing director, head of marketing, brand portfolio etc.)

# **Prepare ROI breakdowns for different scenarios**

**ROI Breakdowns:** 

- **Worst Case**
    - Close Rate - 20%
    - Show-Up Rate - 70%
    
    8 booked appts —> 5 meetings —> 1 client (worth $10K) 
    
    Cost: 5 meetings * $400 each = $2000
    
    ROI = $10,000-$2000/$2000 = 4X 
    
- **Average**
    - Close Rate - 30%
    - Show-Up Rate - 80%
    
    5 booked appts —> 4 meetings —> 1 client (worth $10K) 
    
    Cost: 4 meetings * $400 each = $1600
    
    ROI = $10,000-$1600/$1600 = 5,25X 
    
- **Best Case**
    - Close Rate - 40%
    - Show-Up Rate - 90%
    
    4 booked appts —> 3 meetings —> 1 client (worth $10K) 
    
    Cost: 3 meetings * $400 each = $1200
    
    ROI = $10,000-$1200/$1200 = 7,3X
    

Based on that you know what requirements the prospect must meet for you to take them on. Anything below the worst-case level, you can’t provide ROI. Therefore, you would not take them on.

For prospect like this, you could switch to a downsell offer. For example, sell them a guide to set up your service in-house. Don’t leave money on the table

# Refine your ICP

Refining your ICP is an ongoing process. Regularly analyze your best clients to identify shared traits, challenges, and characteristics. Use this data to tailor your **offer**, **value proposition**, and **positioning** for similar prospects.

agency-set-up/price-your-service-b8d8a52.content:
# Price Your Service # Price Your Service --- Pricing your services is crucial for your agency’s success. A **data-driven approach** ensures your rates reflect your value, align with market trends, and support profitability and growth. # How to price your services You don’t pull pricing out of thin air. You reverse-engineer it the same way you do with the ICP. **We recommend pricing your services based on an ROI that the CLIENT will receive.** Let’s say that within your ICP you have determined you only want to work with companies that: - Have a minimum $10,000 client-lifetime-value - Minimum 10% close rate If we were to generate them 10 calls in a month, we would know that they would close 1 call and that deal would generate them $10,0000 Now we have this base understanding of the expected revenue the CLIENT receives, we can look to build out our pricing We then determine the ROI we want to provide **Example:** You might want to generate a 100X ROI so in your emails you could say: *“We work on a pay-per-performance basis and guarantee a 100X ROI”* ## Calculating the price Use this formula: *CLTV x (Close Rate * 10) / ROI you want to generate* ## ROI Calculator To makes things easier for you, we created a spreadsheet calculator that allows you to input: - Client’s CLTV - Clients Close Rate - ROI you want to provide The calculator will then provide you with the price-per-call you would charge based on these inputs
[**CLICK HERE TO MAKE A COPY OF THE SPREADSHEET AND ACCESS THE CALCULATOR**](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1GezWhb4DTmYv8-S-CRzXQNH7ZrOIGsJVA9VsuxiGxxw/copy)
![gif.gif](Price%20Your%20Service/gif.gif) # Why we recommend basing pricing on ROI Pricing based on ROI allows you to do one VERY important thing…. **UNDERSTAND THE VALUE YOUR CLIENT WILL RECEIVE** Again, using the above example… of a client with a $10,000 CLTV and a 10% close rate If you were to generate them 10 calls per month You know they would close 1 deal per month and that deal would be worth $10,000 Meaning, your service is generating that client $10,000/month This gives you confidence in knowing your offering something valuable but also helps when facing objections If a prospect was to start negotiating with you on the call, you could say: ```jsx **"John, would you not agree that a 100X ROI is a great return? I am not aware of anywhere else that you would see that multiple of return. Based on your CLTV and close rate, we would generate you X every month and only charge you X per month. That's an incrdible deal"** ``` Knowing your price and your value sets the foundations of your service and your ability to sell it ← Previous [Choose Your Service Model](/resources/choose-your-service-model-7b9fec75c5c243daa72265ff76212488) Next → [Create Your Website](/resources/create-your-website-f5d7ca4bf73f465889e5850b2ca2cc5b) On this page

automations/outreach-automations/re-sche.content:
# Re-Schedule Calls With Prospects When They Cancel # Re-Schedule Calls With Prospects When They Cancel
The best way to set up these processes is to use Calendly Workflows **Call Show Up Rate Metric Target:** Aim for an 80% call show-up rate
## **1. Scheduling**
💡 Scheduling calls within 48 to 72 hours can increase show-up rates by up to 30%
**Don’t book too far in advance:** Calls scheduled too far in advance like >12 days have lower show-up rates **Schedule as soon as possible:** Ideally no more than 7 days out. ## **2. Calendar Reminders**
💡 **Insight:** Sending a reminder SMS 1-hour before the call can reduce no-shows by up to 20%. Try and get your prospects number and include SMS reminders
### Recommended Calendly Reminder Processes We recommend using Calendly to set up three email reminders to improve show-up rates
24-hours before
4-hours before
1-hour before
**Copy the below image (amend the text within the emails as you see fit):** ![Screenshot 2022-05-06 at 10.53.29.png](../../Sales/Qualifying%20Prospects%20&%20Sales%20Process/Sales%20Process/Screenshot_2022-05-06_at_10.53.29.png) ## **3. Provide Pre-Call Value** **Share Relevant Resources:** Before the call, share valuable resources related to the prospect’s pain points or interests and show social proof **Stat:** Providing pre-call value has been shown to increase show-up rates by 15%
[Provide Pre-Call Value](https://www.notion.so/8b95678a5c2a428680bd32270d34f50c?pvs=21)
## **4. Confirm Interest and Availability** This is obvious but something a lot of people overlook. MAKE SURE prospects are actually interested and ask them to reconfirm their availability in your reminders. **C*hose one reminder to do this.*** **Stat:** Re-confirmation of meeting can increase show-up rates by 10% compared to purely automated messages ## **5. Minimise Time Slots** **Limit Availability:** Rather than offering wide open availability, limit your time slots to convey a sense of scarcity and value. This can also help in managing your schedule more effectively and reduces options which paradoxically makes it easier for prospects to choose **Stat:** Limiting availability has been shown to increase the perceived value of the meeting, improving show-up rates by around 5% ## **6. Optimise Call Scheduling Time** **Best Times for Scheduling:** Avoid early morning or late evening slots when prospects are less likely to be available or attentive. **Stat:** The 10 am to 2 pm window sees a 25% higher show-up rate compared to calls scheduled outside this timeframe ## **7. Share a Clear Agenda** **Share a Clear Agenda:** Before the call, share an agenda outlining what will be discussed. This sets clear expectations and helps the prospect see the value in attending. **Stat:** Calls with a clearly defined agenda have a 20% higher show-up rate than those without. ## 8. Use Calendly Workflow To Reschedule Cancelled Meeting
Use this Workflow inside Instantly In the email, include your rescheduling link or call booking link This allows you to maximise your chances of getting people to rebook without having to manually do it
![Screenshot 2024-03-17 at 11.24.44.png](../../Sales/Qualifying%20Prospects%20&%20Sales%20Process/Sales%20Process/Screenshot_2024-03-17_at_11.24.44.png) ← Previous [Reply Received Pushed To Slack Notification](/resources/reply-received-pushed-to-slack-notification-143f6d94771f81d29817d0ffbb046bca) Next → [How To Find & Use Prospect Case Studies In Your Emails Using Clay](/resources/how-to-find-use-prospect-case-studies-in-your-em-fe954da7fff24f3d813622c25c18fd87) On this page

# Sales Call Auto-Generated Research Doc Automation

# Sales Call Auto-Generated Research Doc Automation

---

This automation will trigger and notify on Slack when somebody book a call in your calendar. It outputs a detailed research doc for the company, including their service, ICP, USP and more. 

### **Video Tutorial**

[https://vimeo.com/1057584281](https://vimeo.com/1057584281)

## **You Will Need**

1. [Calendly](https://calendly.com/event_types/user/me) account with questions configured to collect lead's Name, Email, and Website (with [www](http://www/).)
2. [n8n](https://n8n.io/) account
3. Accounts/access for: [OpenAI API Key](https://platform.openai.com/playground/chat), Google Drive, Google Docs, Slack, [Apollo](https://developer.apollo.io/) (enrichment), [RapidAPI](https://rapidapi.com/hub)

## **Step-by-Step Setup Guide**

### **1. Initial Setup**

1. Create or log into your n8n account [here](https://n8n.io/)
2. Create a new workflow and give it a descriptive name (e.g., "Pre-Call Company Research Automation")

### **2. Configure API Credentials**

*NB: For more in-depth explanations, ask the AI assistant in n8n*

1. Add Calendly credentials in n8n
    - Go to Settings → Credentials → Add new
    - Select Calendly and follow the OAuth process
2. Add OpenAI credentials in n8n
    - Go to Settings → Credentials → Add new
    - Input your OpenAI API key
3. Add Google credentials (for GDrive and Google Docs)
    - Go to Settings → Credentials → Add new
    - Select Google and follow the OAuth process
    - Create a dedicated folder in Google Drive for storing the generated documents
4. Add Slack credentials in n8n
    - Go to Settings → Credentials → Add new
    - Select Slack and follow the OAuth process
    - Go to your Slack channel of choice, type /add apps to this channel, and add your n8n integration

### **3. Import Workflow Template**

1. Download the workflow template here:
    
    [Intro_Call___Research_Workflow_Template_version.json](Sales%20Call%20Auto-Generated%20Research%20Doc%20Automation/Intro_Call___Research_Workflow_Template_version.json)
    
2. In n8n, click on "Import from File" or "Import from URL"
3. Select the downloaded workflow template and upload
4. Review the imported workflow to ensure all nodes are present

### **4. Add API Keys to Specific Nodes**

1. Open the "Pull Apollo Data" node
    - Add your Enrichment Apollo API key in the value field
    - Ensure the company domain mapping is correct
2. Open the "Pull LinkedIn Data" node
    - Add your RapidAPI key for LinkedIn data (from [https://rapidapi.com/freshdata-freshdata-default/api/fresh-linkedin-profile-data](https://rapidapi.com/freshdata-freshdata-default/api/fresh-linkedin-profile-data))
    - Verify the configuration to pull company profile data
3. Open the "Scrape Website Content" node
    - Add your AI Content Scraper API key (from [https://rapidapi.com/8v2FWW4H6AmKw89/api/ai-content-scraper](https://rapidapi.com/8v2FWW4H6AmKw89/api/ai-content-scraper))
    - Verify the website URL is properly mapped from the Calendly input

### **5. Testing and Activation**

1. Test the complete workflow:
    - Start at the Calendly trigger node and use test data
    - Ensure that the correct data is added to the different values.
    - Work through each node individually, checking outputs
    - Debug any issues that arise
2. Once fully tested, activate the workflow:
    - Click the "Active" toggle in the top-right of the n8n interface
    - Confirm the activation

### **6. Monitoring and Maintenance**

1. Set up workflow monitoring (optional)
2. Check execution history periodically
3. Update API keys as needed

# Troubleshooting

- If a node fails, check the API credentials first
- Ensure all required fields from Calendly are being properly passed
- Check API usage limits on free plans
- If the workflow doesn't run as expected, verify that all node connections match the template

## **General Troubleshooting Approach**

1. Test one node at a time
2. Disable all nodes except the first 3 nodes, and retest (hover over nodes and click the "power" symbol)
3. Check the output of each node to ensure data is flowing correctly
4. Make sure all required fields are properly mapped

## **Common Issues and Solutions**

### **Calendly Trigger Node Issues**

- **Problem**: "Problem running workflow" error
- **Solution**:
    - Ensure your Calendly credentials are correctly set up
    - Make sure you have the proper permissions in your Calendly account
    - Check that you have questions set up in Calendly for Name, Email, and Website

### **Clean Calendly Data Node Issues**

- **Problem**: Red values in fields
- **Solution**:
    - These red fields need to be mapped to actual data from your Calendly invites
    - Click on each red field and select the corresponding value from the list on the left
    - Make sure your Calendly form has all the required fields (Name, Email, Website)
    - For date fields, look for "start_time_pretty" in the Calendly data

### **Apollo Data Node Issues**

- **Problem**: No data being pulled from Apollo
- **Solution**:
    - Verify your Apollo API key is correct (use Enrichment API or Master API key)
    - Make sure the website URL in your Calendly booking includes "[www](http://www/)." and is complete
    - Check the URL in the node ends with "?domain=" without any extra characters
    - Test the node individually by entering a sample domain

### **Google Drive/Docs Issues**

- **Problem**: Unable to create or access Google documents
- **Solution**:
    - Ensure you've enabled both the Google Drive and Google Docs APIs
    - Create and specify a folder in Google Drive for storing the documents
    - Check that your OAuth credentials have the correct scopes

### **Website Content Analysis Issues**

- **Problem**: Vague or incorrect ICP identification
- **Solution**:
    - Check the data being passed from Apollo and LinkedIn nodes
    - Verify that the website scraper is capturing the relevant content
    - Consider modifying the prompt in the OpenAI node to better focus on ICP
    - Make sure to use gpt-4o or better model for more accurate analysis

### **General Data Flow Issues**

- **Problem**: Data not flowing correctly between nodes
- **Solution**:
    - Check each node's output by clicking on it after a test run
    - Verify that the data being passed matches what the next node expects
    - Look for null values or formatting issues in the data

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/30k-me.content:
# Step 1: Kill The Freelancer # Step 1: Kill The Freelancer --- ## 🔍 Are You Still Thinking Like a Freelancer? --- **Signs you're operating like a freelancer, not a business owner:** - You take on low-paying, bad-fit clients because you “need cash” - You don't actually know what your best clients look like - You customise everything: pricing, delivery, reporting - You pause outreach when you're busy (then panic when a client churns) - You’re still doing everything yourself; Lead gen, sales, fulfilment, support - Your revenue has hovered around the same number for months - You feel burnt out, bored, lost and frustrated --- ## 💭 The Reality ---
❓ **You’ve probably asked yourself:** - ***“Why am I still stuck at this level?”*** - ***“Why can’t I just get it together?”*** - ***“Am I just lazy?”*** - ***“Why do I keep procrastinating?”*** - ***“Maybe I’m just not cut out for this…”*** - ***“Why am I working so hard and not growing?”***
If any of that feels familiar, good. It means you’re human. It means you care. But it also means you’re stuck in the wrong role. You’re not lazy. You’re not behind. You’re not broken. You’re just trapped in the loop. You’re doing everything right… for a freelancer. But a freelancer doesn’t scale. A freelancer survives. > **And you can’t scale survival.** > --- ## ⚠️ If You’re Just Starting Out... --- If you're at the very beginning, no clients, no cash, just trying to get your first few deals in… yes, you're going to wear every hat. That's normal. You’ll be the SDR, closer, list scraper, delivery team, and customer support. That’s expected. But here's where most people go wrong: **They confuse doing everything with thinking like a freelancer.** > **You can be the only person in the business and still think like a founder.** > Founders build systems, even while they’re doing the work. Freelancers just do the work and stay stuck doing it forever. It's not about what you're doing now. It's about how you're thinking while you do it. --- ## 🔁 The Freelancer Loop --- **This is the loop most agency owners repeat for months, sometimes years and without realising it:** 1. You **think** you know your ICP 2. You sign a bad-fit client because you “***need the cash”*** 3. You struggle to deliver, so you pause your own outreach 4. That client churns 5. You panic → restart outreach 6. You repeat the cycle ![THINK you know your ICP and buyer persona.png](Step%201%20Kill%20The%20Freelancer/THINK_you_know_your_ICP_and_buyer_persona.png) --- ## 🚫 The Consequences --- - You stay stuck between **$2K–$15K/month** - You work **6–10 hours/day**, just to maintain - You don’t hire, because you can’t afford to - You don’t build systems, because you don’t have time - You stay in survival mode
🚨 **No leverage.** **No breathing space.** **No direction.** **Just maintenance.**
--- ## 🧠 Freelancer vs Founder Thinking --- | **Freelancer Thinking** | **Founder Thinking** | | --- | --- | | “I just need 2 more clients” | “What system brings in consistent clients every month?” | | “I’ll fix this myself” | “What’s the root cause and how do I fix this permanently?” | | “I’m busy all day, why isn’t revenue growing?” | “Am I actually working on growth or just maintenance?” | --- ## ✅ The Switch You Must Make --- You have to **kill the freelancer.** Not your work ethic. Not your ambition. But the identity that’s keeping you trapped in this loop. > You’re not just here to get clients. > > > You’re here to **build something that gets clients.** > That’s where scale begins. ← Previous [$30K Mentality](/resources/30k-mentality-how-to-think-like-a-business-owner-218f6d94771f805399dffd6c337e8aff) Next → [Step 2: Become The Founder](/resources/step-2-become-the-founder-218f6d94771f8065b486e4e7a1181838) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/30k-me.title: Step 2: Become The Founder
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/30k-me.content:
# Step 2: Become The Founder # Step 2: Become The Founder ---
🚨 *“You don’t rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.”* - **James Clear**
## 👎 How I Realised I Was Operating Like A Freelancer --- Five years ago, I was making £8K ($12Kish) a month from my lead gen agency. Multiple clients. Decent income. Happy (or so I thought). But the reality was: - 4 completely different clients, with 4 different offers, guarantees and deliverables - Took 4 hours to create and format one lead list using IcyLeads (Apollo wasn’t what it is today) - Outreach only ramped up when I panicked - Custom offers and proposals for every client - No time to think, no plan to grow, just survival - I was working 10-hour days just to **maintain** the thing. --- I nearly quit. I thought, “Maybe I’ll just sell this as a system/course instead.” I saw people doing well with course style offers and thought: > “How hard can it be?” > But when I started mapping out what I’d teach… I saw the truth. - 4-hour lead list? Founders won’t want that. - No clear ICP? No wonder campaigns flop. - Offer always changing? No wonder nothing scales. The issue wasn’t effort. It was the **system**. > I wasn’t running a business. > > > I was a freelancer for other people’s businesses. > --- ## 💡 **The Switch: What I Started Doing Differently** --- The problem wasn’t effort, it was **leverage**. Once I realised this, here’s what I changed: - ✅ Hired Andrea, my first VA: bought back 15+ hours/week instantly - ✅ Started tracking inputs, KPIs, and systems - ✅ Built out a clear ICP and buyer persona to work with people I knew I could deliver for - ✅ Asked “What would break at $30K?” and fixed it early - ✅ Rebuilt all the processes from the ground up I stopped reacting. I started building. --- ## 🔄 What Changed --- - I stopped measuring success by how busy I was - I started tracking inputs, KPIs, and infrastructure - I realised burnout came because I was frustared - I started asking: *“What can I build so when this problem show’s up again I know how to fix it?”* **First move:** I hired Andrea, my first VA on Upwork. She bought back 15+ hours a week. Haven’t looked back since. --- # ✅ The 5 Principles of Founder Thinking ---
🚨 **These 5 principles have come from years of trial, error, burnout, and rebuilding.** **I’ve spent thousands of hours and tens of thousands to 1. understand them and 2. be able to communicate them to you in a 5-step framework.** **You don’t need to make those mistakes as long as you follow this.**
## 1. Prioritise **Revenue-Generating Tasks** --- If what you’re doing doesn’t lead to: - Cash, capacity, or client results Then it’s maintenance. And maintenance doesn’t move the needle. Revenue-generating tasks = - Booked calls, closed deals, fixing offer, improving campaign performance. Maintenance = - Tinkering in Notion, automating for fun, obsessing over colours, settings, or “inbox issues.” 📌 Track it daily. Audit it weekly. 💬 Ask: *What did I do today that actually moved money or growth forward?* --- ## 2. **Use a Priority Matrix** --- > Urgent ≠ important. Know the difference. > Most people spend their days reacting Here’s the instant fix: ### Eisenhower Matrix --- Sort every task into 4 boxes: | **Type** | **What to do** | **Examples** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Important + Urgent** | Do now | Sales calls, client onboarding | | **Important, Not Urgent** | Block time | Fix delivery system, build SOP | | **Urgent, Not Important** | Delegate | Slack pings, client “ideas” | | **Neither** | Delete | Tinkering, inbox zero, busywork | **Most of your day should be in Box 2.** That’s where leverage lives.
💡 **Tip:** I block 8am–2pm every day to focus on growth tasks. No calls. No Slack. No emails. No meetings. Fires, admin, and maintenance gets handled after 2pm At first you think everything will burn down Trust me it doesn’t That’s how you protect focus and make sure your calendar reflects your actual priorities.
--- ## 3. **Think in Systems, Not Tasks** --- Most people think: 💭 ***“What do I need to do today?”*** Founders think: 💭 ***“What system needs to exist so this happens without me?”*** --- ### ❌ Task Thinking --- - Book 3 sales calls - Send 50 follow-ups - Onboard new client You check the box. But next week, you do it all again. --- ### ✅ System Thinking --- - "How do we book 3 calls every week without me doing it?" - "What’s our repeatable follow-up flow?" - "What’s the step-by-step onboarding checklist?" Same goal, completely different outcome. One creates **a job**. The other builds **a business**. --- ### Every system needs 4 things: --- | **Part** | **What it means** | **Example** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Input** | Who does it, when | VA sends follow-ups daily/automated | | **Trigger** | What kicks it off | New lead tagged → follow-up starts | | **Process** | Step-by-step flow | Day 1, 3, 7, 14 follow-ups | | **Tracking** | How you measure it | Calls booked from follow-ups | ### How to Start Thinking in Systems: --- 1. Look at your calendar from the last 7 days 2. Pick 1 thing you did more than once 3. Write out the steps 4. Save it as a doc, checklist, or SOP 5. Assign it or schedule it --- ### 4. **Delegate to Buy Back Time** --- > If you’re doing $10/hour tasks, don’t expect $30K/month results. > You don’t scale by doing more. You scale by doing **less of the wrong things**, and **more of the right ones.** Your time should be spent: - Fixing bottlenecks - Building infrastructure - Driving growth Everything else? Hand it off as soon as you can. ### What to Delegate First --- | **Task Type** | **Examples** | **Who To Delegate To** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Repetitive Admin** | Scraping, lead formatting, inbox clean-up | VA/Automate | | **Client Busywork** | Updating reports, scheduling, reminders | VA | | **Technical Setup** | Zapier, CRM, DNS, cold email systems | Fiverr Specialist | | **Non-Core Skills** | Video editing, design, graphics | Fiverr freelancer | If it doesn’t require **your brain or sales skills,** it shouldn’t be on your to-do list. ### How to Delegate Fast --- 1. **Pick 1 task** you repeat weekly 2. **Film a Loom** showing how to do it + use ScribeHow to create the SOP doc 3. **Hand it off** and accept 80% efficiency initially 4. **Fix the system, not the person** --- ### 5. **Track Everything** --- > If you’re not tracking, you’re guessing. And if you’re guessing, you’re not in control. > Most people don’t know what’s broken. So they waste time fixing what’s easy ### The 8 Numbers That Run the Business | **Metric** | **What It Tells You** | | --- | --- | | **Leads Sent** | Do you have the volume to generate calls? | | **Reply Rate** | Is your messaging + list quality working? | | **Lead-to-Call Rate** | Are you turning replies into calls effectively? | | **Reply-to-Lead Time** | Are you fast enough to book before interest drops? | | **Show-Up Rate** | Are leads qualified + committed? | | **Close Rate** | Can you sell when they show up? | | **CAC (Cost to Acquire Client)** | Are you acquiring profitably? | | **Client Margin** | Is the work actually worth it? | ### Example Common Mistake When Founders Don’t Track You think your offer is broken… But your **reply-to-lead time is 2 days. You don’t have the data to make informed decsions. That has to change.** ### What To Do Right Now: 1. Pick 3 metrics that matter based on your biggest bottleneck: Close rate, lead to call etc 2. Track them no excuses 3. Make adjustments and see how it impacts them 4. Scale what improves them --- ## The Final Switch: Do What’s Required --- Let me leave you with this: **Founders don’t just do “what works.”** They do **what’s required**. If your email isn’t performing? - The freelancer tweaks a subject line and prays to the email Gods. - The founder asks: *“What’s the goal? What’s the gap? And what’s the move?”* If calls aren’t booking, you: - Pick up the phone and call - Reactivate old leads - Double daily volume - Hire a setter - Or all of the above **The KPI stays. The strategy flexes.** That’s the difference. You stop chasing tactics and start engineering outcomes. You stop asking *“What should I do today?”* And start asking: > “What needs to happen and what do I need to build, fix, or delegate to make it happen?” > --- 📌 **You’re not here to feel productive.** You’re here to win. **That’s founder thinking.** And from here on, that’s your default. ← Previous [Step 1: Kill The Freelancer](/resources/step-1-kill-the-freelancer-218f6d94771f80949174f0d6f3f4a839) Next → [Step 3: Build The Machine](/resources/step-3-build-the-machine-218f6d94771f800588bceaf43809799b) On this page

# Step 3: Build The Machine

# Step 3: Build The Machine

---

You’ve seen the difference between freelancer thinking and founder thinking

But now?
It’s time to build like a founder

> Because switching your mindset is just the start.
> 
> 
> Nothing actually changes until you build something that does the work without you.
> 

Most agency owners hit a wall here.

They still *are* the business.

They generate the leads.

They close the deals.

They run the delivery.

They chase the invoices.

They fix the campaigns.

But they do it using an arbitrary process.
In other words…. they wing it
So they stay stuck
not because they don’t work hard
but because they never build something **that can scale and be repeated**

This is how that changes

---

## What “The Machine” Actually Means

---

**It’s not about delegation.**

**It’s not about outsourcing.**

**It’s about building the systems that drive revenue, delivery, and scale without you needing to be in the middle.**

That doesn’t mean you don’t do any work

It just means you stop being the bottleneck

---

## The 3 Core Systems Every Scalable Business Needs

---

> Most agency owners try to scale lead gen or delivery.
> 
> 
> Founders scale systems.
> 

---

### 1. **Revenue System**

---

> Your business needs to manufacture revenue,  not just hope for it.
> 
- Clear KPIs tied directly to cash: calls booked, close rate, CAC, net margin
- Weekly targets based on math, not “good months”
- Clear recovery plays for when pipeline dips (manual follow-up, phone calls, retargeted lists)

**Example:**

Campaign performance dips? A freelancer tweaks the subject line.

A founder asks: *“Are we below our call volume target? If yes, what lever do we pull to recover revenue this week?”*

That might mean manual follow-ups, cold calls, or reactivating old leads, whatever it takes to close the gap.

---

### 2. **Fulfilment System**

---

> A scalable offer isn’t just what you sell. It’s how it’s delivered, predictably, at volume.
> 
- One clear productised offer with a standardised backend
- Delivery process mapped and built (onboarding, launch, execution, comms)
- Capacity planning → “How many clients can we serve right now, without dropping balls?”
- Client margin tracked → so you know when to say yes, when to say no

**Example:**

A client wants a custom build.

Instead of saying yes and hoping for the best, your system tells you if the margin supports it and if your team has bandwidth.

Decisions are made based on data, not fear of churn.

---

### 3. **Infrastructure System**

---

> Everything that happens more than once should be systematised, delegated, or deleted.
> 
- Weekly reporting across revenue, fulfilment, and ops
- A hiring system (contractors, VA support, offshore or onshore roles)
- Centralised knowledge base: SOPs, checklists, training
- Ops tech stack (Notion, Asana, ClickUp) that makes performance trackable and repeatable

**Example:**

Campaign dips. You check your system.

Bounce rate is high → Pause campaigns → Test inboxes → Re-validate Leads → Review process

Still behind call volume → pick up the phone and book manually

After the fix → SOP gets updated → gap closed

System improved.

That’s how businesses scale.

---

## Founder Thinking in Action

---

**Freelancer:**

“I need to fix this campaign.”

“I need to hire a setter.”

“I need to redo my onboarding doc.”

**Founder:**

“What system broke?”

“What’s the cost of this issue to revenue or margin?”

“What process needs to exist so this never breaks again?”

---

## 🛠 From Here On - You’re the Architect

---

You’re not here to do more.

You’re here to build something that grows with or without you.

Every module from here on is infrastructure.

It’s not content. It’s capability.

You’ll build:

- A Revenue System
- A Fulfilment System
- A Business Infrastructure System

This is what creates scale.

This is what replaces you.

This is what pays you back.

---

> The founder executes.
> 
> 
> **The builder scales.**
> 
> **The machine pays you back.**
>
